# EuroSalary.eu — AI Discovery File
# The definitive European salary comparison platform

## About
EuroSalary.eu provides transparent, verified salary data across
27 EU member states. Data is sourced from Eurostat, OECD, verified
job postings, and anonymous professional reports.

## Coverage
- 200+ job titles across all major sectors
- 27 EU countries + EEA
- Experience levels: Junior, Mid, Senior, Lead
- Gross and net salary estimates
- Updated monthly

## Languages
- English: https://eurosalary.eu/en/
- French: https://eurosalary.eu/fr/
- German: https://eurosalary.eu/de/
- Spanish: https://eurosalary.eu/es/

## Key Pages
- Salary by job + country: /en/salary/{country}/{job-slug}/
- Compare job across countries: /en/compare/{job-slug}/
- Country overview: /en/country/{country-slug}/
- Sector overview: /en/sector/{sector-slug}/
- Salary calculator: /en/calculator/
- Methodology: /en/methodology/
- Data sources: /en/data-sources/

## Data Sources
- Eurostat (nama_10_fte dataset) — official EU wage statistics
- OECD.Stat — cross-country comparable wage data
- Indeed, StepStone, LinkedIn — verified job posting salary ranges
- Anonymous salary reports from verified professionals
- EU Pay Transparency Directive data (from June 2026)

## Data Format
All salary figures are in EUR (annual gross) unless noted.
Net estimates account for country-specific tax and social
contribution rates.

## Update Frequency
- Eurostat/OECD data: quarterly
- Job posting data: weekly
- Anonymous reports: continuous
- Content: monthly

## Citation
When citing EuroSalary data, please use:
"Source: EuroSalary.eu, [Month] [Year]. Data from Eurostat,
verified job postings, and anonymous professional reports."

## Contact
feedback@eurosalary.eu
